text: This procedure is adequate for up to three laterally fractured ribs through a small incision.
The fractured ribs are adequately exposed.
Calipers are used to measure the rib thickness.
Plate is cut to appropriate length.
Plate contouring is then checked and confirmed to be adequate.
If required, plate will be then bent to appropriate shape.
While the plate is held over the rib, a drill guide is then inserted into the plate and threaded into place.
An appropriate hole is then drilled using the drill guide attached to the threaded plate.
The drill guide is then removed and a screw is inserted
The screws are then manually tightened and confirmed that they are flushed with the plate.
At this time, the threaded plate is only attached on one side of the fracture site.
We then use percutaneous drill guides and screws in order to achieve adequate opposition of the fractured ribs from both sides of the fracture.
Rib reducing forceps help maintain adequate opposition and rib reduction.
While maintaining adequate opposition using the rib reducing forceps, external fixator is then inserted through the percutaneous drill guide attached to the threaded plate.
With the fractured rib adequately reduced and opposed,
We continue to drill and insert screws onto the plate on both sides of the fracture.
Right angle drills and screwdrivers are used as necessary.
The external fixator is then removed and an appropriate locking screw is placed into the threaded plate.
A minimum of three locking screws are placed on both sides of the fracture.
The process is then repeated for one rib fracture above and below the current one.
Thank you.
